work_package_result.summary: Added a secret-safe Codex SDK 0.144.5 canary that uses a dedicated Codex-managed ChatGPT subscription login, proves two ordered streamed turns on the same observed thread, and isolates normal user auth, configuration, Git state and project content.

passed: 19 deterministic Node adapter tests cover auth mode, exact versions, environment isolation, event shape and order, continuation identity, deadlines, late cleanup reconciliation and secret-safe failures
live canary through ChatGPT subscription: SDK and CLI 0.144.5, first turn completed in 16846 ms, continuation completed in 11027 ms with sameThread true
production dependency audit reported no known vulnerabilities
just check: repository, docs, planning, CI metadata, generated catalogue, Rust fmt/clippy/tests, 53 Python tests, workspace TypeScript and protocol compatibility
git diff --check and staged secret/path review
not_run: live subscription calls remain an explicit manual canary and are not part of normal CI
physical cancellation of an in-flight Node filesystem syscall is unavailable; timeout paths fail closed and schedule one bounded reconciliation pass

review.method: R2 detached read-only review through an isolated Codex SDK model context using line-numbered bounded source input; successive findings were repaired and the final exact cleanup re-review returned R2 GATE: PASS
findings_closed: Pinned built-in OpenAI provider, exact ChatGPT login output and SDK/CLI 0.144.5 while stripping API-key, custom-provider and injected-token environment
Moved authentication to a dedicated official Codex ChatGPT login so normal Codex auth is never read, linked or modified
Isolated per-run CODEX_HOME, HOME, Git configuration and empty Git workspace from user config, plugins, skills, MCP and project content
Validated runtime event ordering and field shapes, rejected tool, file, MCP, web and unknown item classes, and normalized all printed failure details
Bounded Git, filesystem, turn and iterator cleanup; setup and cleanup timeouts fail closed and schedule one non-recursive late reconciliation pass
Proved continuation from the observed streamed thread identifier rather than an assumed SDK object property
Made repository JSON validation use tracked canonical files instead of ignored dependency and build trees

observability_changes: canary emits only auth mode, normalized SDK/CLI versions, event kinds, terminal state, agent-message count, same-thread evidence and latency
authentication, configuration, model, network, rate-limit, service and tooling failures map to fixed safe classifications without provider payloads
known_limitations: The dedicated canary ChatGPT login is local development state and may require an occasional official browser re-login
Exact English CLI status and version matching intentionally fail closed after upstream wording or version changes
Node filesystem operations are not physically cancellable; timed-out mutations are reported as cleanup failure and receive one bounded late reconciliation pass
@modelcontextprotocol/sdk remains a development-only type-resolution workaround for the pinned Codex SDK declaration package
